NOTICE OF UNAVAILABILITY OF SALAZAR, JR., JOSEPH A. ON DEC-23-2013 THROUGH JAN-10-2014 FILED BY DEFENDANT BRADSHAW, ROBERT AKA BOB BRADSHAW

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFFS ULRIKE SILKEY'S AND DAVID SILKEY'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER COMPELLING FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES AND FOR AN ORDER FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED AS MOOT. DEFENDANT'S INITIAL RESPONSE TO FORM INTERROGATORY 15.1, CONSISTING SOLELY OF OBJECTIONS, WAS IMPROPER AND CONSTITUTES A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. A PARTY MAY NOT EVADE ITS RESPONSIBILITY TO RESPOND TO FORM INTERROGATORY 15.1 WITH FACTS SIMPLY BY ASSERTING THAT ITS INVESTIGATION AND DISCOVERY ARE NOT COMPLETE. NEVERTHELESS, DEFENDANT HAS SUPPLEMENTED ITS RESPONSES AFTER THE FILING OF PLAINTIFFS' MOTION, AND PLAINTIFFS' REPLY BRIEF DOES NOT CHALLENGE THE ADEQUACY OF THE SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES. REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM ERNEST A. HALPERIN, CLERK K. LIU, NOT REPORTED. (D302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Compelling Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And For An Order For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-15-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS ULRIKE SILKEY AND DAVID SILKEY'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER COMPELLING FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES AND FOR AN ORDER FOR SANCTIONS

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: This motion is transferred to the Discovery calendar, to be heard at 9:00 am on the same date in Department 302. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: E. Larnauti; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185 (302/MJM)

Plaintiffs Ulrike Silkey'S And David Silkey'S Motion For An Order Compelling Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And For An Order For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-15-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances, Atty. Rowena P. Lizin of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP (415-438-6617) appear for defendant Robert Bradshaw moving party. No other appearance.

The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiffs Ulrike Silkey'S And David Silkey'S Motion For An Order Compelling Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And For An Order For Sanctions is DENIED AS MOOT. Defendant's initial response to Form Interrogatory 15.1, consisting solely of objections, was improper and constitutes a misuse of the discovery process. A party may not evade its responsibility to respond to Form Interrogatory 15.1 with facts simply by asserting that its investigation and discovery are not complete. Nevertheless, Defendant has supplemented its responses after the filing of Plaintiffs' motion, and Plaintiffs' reply brief does not challenge the adequacy of the supplemental responses. Request for sanctions is Denied. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem Ernest A. Halperin, Clerk K. Liu, not reported. (D302)

Defendant Robert Bradshaw'S Motion To Quash Plaintiff'S Subpoena To David Pine, M.D.

HEARING - R U L I N G: Defendant ROBERT BRADSHAW'S Motion To Quash Plaintiff's Subpoena To David Pine, M.D. is argued and conditionally Granted. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem: William E. Shapiro; Clerk L. Fong; matter not reported. (302/JPT)
